title: Clarify template and adopter repository branch workflows

sources.reason: Repository workflow policy and documentation are the authoritative sources for branch-base and Work Item/PR mapping.
Existing design establishes the one Work Item to one branch and one pull or merge request invariant.
Existing installation and upgrade guidance defines adopter repository behavior.

riskAssessment.reason: Workflow guidance must distinguish the template repository from adopter repositories without assuming an adopter default branch name.

executionDecision.reason: The user confirmed the repository-role distinction and approved implementation.

preReviewWarnings: Confirm that adopter guidance discovers the target repository default branch rather than hard-coding origin/main.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: Repository rules state one Work Item maps to one work branch and one pull or merge request.
Template-maintenance guidance requires the template branch to start from the latest origin/main.
Adopter-project installation and upgrade guidance requires the branch to start from the adopter repository's latest remote default branch, without assuming its name or remote alias.
Adopter guidance pins installation and upgrade to a published template release and records the source release identity in the Work Item.
Documentation distinguishes source/template repository history from adopter-project history and describes post-merge branch cleanup.
guidelines: Keep workflow rules repository-role aware and do not hard-code an adopter branch name.
Keep platform-specific PR metadata separate from repository-local Contract evidence.
Do not modify runtime scripts or CI when documentation and installed agent rules are sufficient.

intent.problem: The repository needs a repeatable one-Work-Item/one-branch/one-PR workflow, but the template repository and an installed adopter repository have different branch authorities. Hard-coding origin/main for both would misdirect adopter work.
constraints: Template maintenance continues to start from the latest origin/main.
Adopter repositories may use any remote name and any default branch name.
Installation and upgrade must use a fixed published template release rather than a moving source branch.
The change is documentation and governance guidance only unless an existing check can be reused without expanding scope.
intent.rationale: Separating repository roles makes the workflow precise, portable, and auditable while preserving the current template release process.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Template-maintenance branch starts from the latest template origin/main.
Adopter-project branch starts from the latest commit on its discovered remote default branch without assuming origin or main.
Adopter installation or upgrade uses a published release tag and one adopter Work Item/PR, followed by branch cleanup.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User explicitly approved implementation of the repository-role-aware workflow rules and documentation.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
